docs.title: Mimir
docs.text:
"And there sat Odin, before the well, and asked counsel of the head of Mimir."

Mimir points at a folder of notes — an Obsidian vault, loose Markdown, PDFs — chunks and embeds them into a local Qdrant vector store, and lets you ask questions about them. Either as fast semantic search, or as a full retrieval-augmented answer from a local Ollama model. Nothing leaves your machine.

docs.title: Local-first & private
docs.text: Runs entirely on your machine. No API keys, no cloud, no tokens leaving your Mac.

docs.title: Semantic search
docs.text: Top-k search across .md, .pdf, .txt, and more — with rich previews and filters.

docs.title: RAG chat
docs.text: Ask questions and get answers grounded in your own notes, with citations back to the source.

docs.title: Native Apple Silicon
docs.text: Embeddings via fastembed (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5, ONNX) — fast, no GPU required.

docs.title: Incremental ingest
docs.text: Re-ingesting skips unchanged files automatically via per-file SHA-256 hashing.

docs.title: Swappable models
docs.text: Change the embedding model or LLM with a single .env value.

docs.title: Where to next
docs.text:
• Quickstart

    Docker, first ingest, and your first query in five commands.

  • CLI reference

    Every command and flag, auto-generated from the source.

  • Architecture

    How ingest, Qdrant, query, and chat fit together.

  • FAQ

    Troubleshooting and common questions.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Mimir is a personal project, but PRs and issues are welcome. This page covers the dev workflow and how to work on the documentation site itself.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/LaurMost/mimir.git
cd mimir
uv sync --extra dev
uv run pre-commit install
docker compose up -d   # start Qdrant for integration work

docs.title: Day-to-day
docs.text:
Command What it does make fmt Ruff format make lint Ruff check uv run pytest Run the test suite uv run mimir <cmd> Run the CLI from your working copy

Pre-commit auto-runs ruff on staged files. If you bypass it, CI will catch the same issues.

docs.title: Branching & commits
docs.text:
• Branch from main. Name branches <type>/<short-slug> — e.g. feat/sparse-vectors, fix/empty-pdf-crash, chore/dependabot-grouping.
  • Conventional commit prefixes: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, ci, deps.
  • Keep commits atomic. A PR should tell one story.

docs.title: Tests
docs.text: Add tests next to existing ones in tests/. The suite is fast and runs without external services thanks to conftest.py isolating the environment. Integration tests that need Qdrant live in tests/test_integration_*.py and only run in CI's integration job.

docs.title: Working on the docs
docs.text:
The documentation is built with Material for MkDocs.

# Install the docs toolchain
uv sync --extra docs

# Live-reload preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000
uv run mkdocs serve

# Production build (fails on broken links / refs)
uv run mkdocs build --strict

Social cards are CI-only

The social plugin (which renders link-preview images) needs the Cairo graphics stack and only runs when the CI environment variable is set. Local mkdocs serve skips it, so you don't need Cairo installed to preview docs.

docs.title: Docs layout
docs.text:
docs/
├── index.md                 # Landing page (custom hero template)
├── getting-started/         # Install, quickstart, configuration
├── guides/                  # Task-focused walkthroughs
├── reference/               # CLI (auto-generated) + architecture
├── blog/                    # Changelog / release posts
├── contributing.md
├── faq.md
├── assets/                  # Logo, favicon, brand.css
└── overrides/home.html      # Custom landing-page template

The CLI reference is generated from mimir.cli:app via mkdocs-typer, so adding or changing a command updates the docs automatically.

docs.title: Deploying the docs
docs.text: Docs deploy automatically. On every push to main, the docs.yml workflow builds the site and runs mkdocs gh-deploy --force, which publishes to the gh-pages branch.

docs.title: One-time GitHub Pages setup
docs.text:
A maintainer needs to enable Pages once:

  1. Push the docs.yml workflow to main (it creates the gh-pages branch on its first successful run).
  2. In the repository, go to Settings → Pages.
  3. Under Build and deployment → Source, choose Deploy from a branch.
  4. Set the branch to gh-pages and the folder to / (root), then save.

The site goes live at https://laurmost.github.io/mimir/ within a minute or two.

docs.title: Releasing
docs.text: See RELEASING.md.

docs.title: Does anything leave my machine?
docs.text: No. Embeddings run locally via fastembed, vectors are stored in a Qdrant container on your machine, and chat uses a local Ollama model. There are no API keys and no outbound calls for ingest, query, or chat.

docs.title: What file types can Mimir ingest?
docs.text: .md, .markdown, .txt, .pdf, .rst, and .org. Other files (images, audio, binaries) are ignored.

docs.title: Where is my data stored?
docs.text: In Qdrant's storage volume, mounted at ./qdrant_storage by the bundled docker-compose.yml. Your original notes are never modified.

docs.title: mimir status says the collection doesn't exist
docs.text: That's expected before your first ingest. Run uv run mimir ingest to create and populate it.

docs.title: Ingest is slow the first time
docs.text: The very first run downloads the fastembed model (a one-time cost) and embeds every chunk. Subsequent ingests are incremental — unchanged files are skipped via SHA-256 hashing.

docs.title: I edited notes but search shows the old content
docs.text: Re-run uv run mimir ingest. Only changed files are re-embedded. To keep things in sync automatically, use mimir watch.

docs.title: How do I force a full re-index?
docs.text:
uv run mimir ingest --force

This bypasses the change-detection hash and re-embeds everything.

docs.title: Query results look irrelevant
docs.text:
A few common causes:

  • The collection was built with a different embedding model than is currently configured. After changing MIMIR_EMBED_MODEL, you must reset and re-ingest.
  • Your MIMIR_CHUNK_SIZE is very large, reducing retrieval precision. Try the defaults.
  • Try increasing --k or removing --folder / --ext filters.

docs.title: mimir chat errors with an Ollama connection problem
docs.text:
Make sure Ollama is running and the model is pulled:

ollama serve
ollama pull qwen2.5:7b-instruct

query works without Ollama — only chat requires it.

docs.title: Can I use a different LLM?
docs.text: Yes. Any model available to Ollama works — set MIMIR_OLLAMA_MODEL. See Local RAG chat.

docs.title: I changed the embedding model and everything broke
docs.text:
Embeddings from different models aren't comparable, and the vector dimensionality must match MIMIR_VECTOR_SIZE. After swapping models:

uv run mimir reset
uv run mimir ingest

docs.title: How do I back up my index?
docs.text:
docker compose stop
tar czf mimir-backup-$(date +%F).tgz qdrant_storage
docker compose start

Or just re-ingest from your notes — embeddings are cheap to regenerate.

docs.title: mimir watch says fswatch not found
docs.text:
Install it:

brew install fswatch

docs.title: Still stuck?
docs.text: Open an issue on GitHub with your OS, Python version, and the output of uv run mimir status.

docs.title: Blog
docs.text: Release notes, roadmap, and the occasional note from building Mimir. For the machine-readable history, see the CHANGELOG.md.

docs.title: Mimir v0.1.0 — the well opens
docs.text: The first release of Mimir is here: a local-first second brain that embeds your notes into a Qdrant vector store on your own machine and lets you query them as semantic search or full RAG chat. Nothing leaves your Mac.

docs.title: What's in it
docs.text:
• Local Qdrant vector store via docker-compose.yml (REST on 6333, gRPC on 6334).
  • Native Apple Silicon embeddings through fastembed with BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 (384d) as the default.
  • A single mimir CLI (Typer) with status, ingest, query, chat, reset, watch, and version.
  • Incremental ingestion — per-file SHA-256 change detection skips unchanged files automatically.
  • Streaming RAG chat via a local Ollama model, with citations back to your notes.
  • Live re-indexing with mimir watch (requires fswatch).
  • Markdown / text / PDF / RST / org loaders, a paragraph-aware chunker, and pre-indexed payload fields for fast filtered search.
  • Configuration from MIMIR_* environment variables and .env.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/LaurMost/mimir.git && cd mimir
uv sync
docker compose up -d
uv run mimir ingest
uv run mimir query "what did I conclude last quarter"

See the Quickstart for the full walkthrough.

docs.title: What's next
docs.text: Sparse and hybrid retrieval, more loaders, and a PyPI release are on the roadmap. Issues and PRs are welcome on GitHub.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
Three short pages take you from nothing to querying your own notes:

  • Installation

    Install Mimir and its prerequisites (Docker, uv, optionally Ollama).

  • Quickstart

    The five-command path from a fresh clone to your first answer.

  • Configuration

    Every MIMIR_* setting, what it does, and sensible defaults.

docs.title: What you'll need
docs.text:
Requirement Why Required? macOS / Linux Mimir is tested on Apple Silicon and Linux Yes Python 3.11+ Runtime Yes Docker (or OrbStack) Runs the Qdrant vector store Yes uv Fast Python package manager Recommended Ollama Local LLM for the chat command Only for chat

On Apple Silicon, OrbStack is lighter than Docker Desktop

brew install --cask orbstack gives you a Docker-compatible runtime that idles at a fraction of the memory.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Mimir reads all configuration from environment variables prefixed with MIMIR_, or from a .env file in the working directory. Every setting has a sensible default, so a zero-config install works out of the box.

Copy the example file to get started:

cp .env.example .env

docs.title: Full reference
docs.text:
All variables, their defaults, and what they control:

Variable Default Description MIMIR_QDRANT_URL http://localhost:6333 URL of the Qdrant instance. MIMIR_COLLECTION mimir Name of the Qdrant collection to read/write. MIMIR_EMBED_MODEL BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 fastembed model used for embeddings. MIMIR_VECTOR_SIZE 384 Dimensionality of the embedding vectors. Must match the model. MIMIR_CHUNK_SIZE 1200 Target chunk size in characters (range 200–8000). MIMIR_CHUNK_OVERLAP 150 Overlap between adjacent chunks in characters (range 0–1000). MIMIR_NOTES_DIR ./notes Default directory ingested when no path is given. MIMIR_BATCH_SIZE 256 Number of points upserted to Qdrant per batch. MIMIR_OLLAMA_URL http://localhost:11434 URL of the Ollama server (used only by chat). MIMIR_OLLAMA_MODEL qwen2.5:7b-instruct Ollama model used for RAG answers.

Validation

MIMIR_CHUNK_SIZE and MIMIR_CHUNK_OVERLAP are validated at startup. Values outside their ranges raise a configuration error rather than silently misbehaving.

docs.title: Example .env
docs.text:
.env
# Qdrant
MIMIR_QDRANT_URL=http://localhost:6333
MIMIR_COLLECTION=mimir

# Embeddings
MIMIR_EMBED_MODEL=BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5
MIMIR_VECTOR_SIZE=384

# Chunking
MIMIR_CHUNK_SIZE=1200
MIMIR_CHUNK_OVERLAP=150

# Ingestion
MIMIR_NOTES_DIR=./notes
MIMIR_BATCH_SIZE=256

# Ollama (only used by `mimir chat`)
MIMIR_OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
MIMIR_OLLAMA_MODEL=qwen2.5:7b-instruct

docs.title: Choosing an embedding model
docs.text:
If you change MIMIR_EMBED_MODEL, you almost certainly need a matching MIMIR_VECTOR_SIZE, and the collection must be recreated:

uv run mimir reset
uv run mimir ingest

Good upgrade paths from the default:

Model Dims Notes BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 384 Default. Fast, good for ≤500k chunks. BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 768 ~2× slower, noticeably better recall. BAAI/bge-m3 1024 Multilingual; dense + sparse + multi-vector. intfloat/multilingual-e5-large 1024 Strong multilingual option.

Changing the model invalidates existing vectors

Embeddings from different models are not comparable. After swapping the model, always reset and re-ingest, otherwise queries return nonsense.

docs.title: Tuning chunking
docs.text:
• Larger MIMIR_CHUNK_SIZE keeps more context together (better for prose and long-form notes) but reduces retrieval precision.
  • Larger MIMIR_CHUNK_OVERLAP reduces the chance of splitting a relevant passage across two chunks, at the cost of more vectors to store.

Defaults (1200 / 150) are a good balance for typical Markdown notes.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Mimir has three moving parts: the Mimir CLI itself, a Qdrant container for vector storage, and (optionally) Ollama for RAG chat.

docs.title: 1. Prerequisites
docs.text:
macOS (Homebrew)Linux 
# Container runtime — OrbStack is lighter than Docker Desktop on M-series
brew install --cask orbstack

# Python package manager + optional local LLM
brew install uv ollama

# Docker (see docs.docker.com for your distro)
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh

# uv
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Ollama (optional, only for `mimir chat`)
curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

docs.title: 2. Install Mimir
docs.text:
Mimir is currently installed from source.

uv (recommended)pip 
git clone https://github.com/LaurMost/mimir.git
cd mimir
uv sync

Run commands with uv run mimir <command>.

git clone https://github.com/LaurMost/mimir.git
cd mimir
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e .

Run commands with mimir <command>.

Why from source?

Mimir is alpha software (v0.1.0). Installing from source keeps you on the latest changes. A PyPI release will follow once the API stabilizes.

docs.title: 3. Start Qdrant
docs.text:
Mimir ships a docker-compose.yml that runs Qdrant with REST on 6333 and gRPC on 6334:

docker compose up -d

Verify it's healthy:

curl -sf http://localhost:6333/healthz && echo "Qdrant is up"

docs.title: 4. (Optional) Pull an Ollama model
docs.text:
Only needed for the chat command:

ollama pull qwen2.5:7b-instruct

docs.title: Verify the install
docs.text:
uv run mimir version
uv run mimir status

status prints your configuration and reports that the collection doesn't exist yet — which is expected before your first ingest.

  • Continue to the Quickstart →

    Ingest your first notes and run a query.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: From a fresh clone to your first answer in about five minutes. This assumes you've already covered the installation prerequisites.

docs.title: The five-command path
docs.text:
# 1. Start the Qdrant vector store
docker compose up -d

# 2. Point Mimir at your notes (symlink a vault, or just drop files in ./notes)
ln -s ~/Documents/Obsidian/MyVault notes/vault

# 3. Ingest — walk, chunk, embed, upsert
uv run mimir ingest

# 4. Ask — fast semantic search
uv run mimir query "what did I conclude about market making on Polymarket"

# 5. (optional) Full RAG chat with a local model
uv run mimir chat "summarise my notes on tailoring fabric sourcing"

That's the whole loop: ingest → query → chat.

docs.title: What just happened
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    notes["notes/ (.md .pdf ...)"] --> ingest["mimir ingest<br/>chunk + embed"]
    ingest --> qdrant[("Qdrant<br/>(Docker)")]
    qdrant --> query["mimir query<br/>(semantic)"]
    qdrant --> chat["mimir chat (RAG)"]
    ollama["Ollama<br/>(local LLM)"] --> chat
  1. Ingest walked your notes directory, split each document into overlapping chunks, embedded them with fastembed, and upserted the vectors into Qdrant.
  2. Query embedded your search text and returned the top-k most similar chunks straight from Qdrant — no LLM involved.
  3. Chat retrieved the relevant chunks and passed them to a local Ollama model as context, returning an answer with citations.

docs.title: Inspect your collection
docs.text:
uv run mimir status
Example output
Qdrant URL              http://localhost:6333
Collection              mimir
Embed model             BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5
Vector size             384
Chunk size / overlap    1200 / 150
Notes dir               ./notes
Points indexed          4,213

docs.title: Re-ingest after editing notes
docs.text:
Re-running ingest is cheap — unchanged files are skipped via a per-file SHA-256 hash, and only modified files are re-embedded:

uv run mimir ingest          # incremental
uv run mimir ingest --force  # re-embed everything

Want it automatic? See Live watch & auto-ingest.

Filter your searches

query supports --folder, --ext, --title, and --k for narrowing results. See the CLI reference.

  • Ingest your Obsidian vault →

    A deeper walkthrough for vault users.

  • Configuration →

    Tune models, chunking, and paths.

docs.title: Guides
docs.text:
Task-focused walkthroughs for getting real work done with Mimir.

  • :material-vault: Ingest your Obsidian vault

    Wire up an existing vault, handle attachments, and keep it in sync.

  • Local RAG chat

    Set up Ollama and ask grounded questions with citations.

  • Live watch & auto-ingest

    Re-ingest automatically whenever you save a note.

docs.title: Ingest your Obsidian vault
docs.text: Obsidian vaults are just folders of Markdown, which makes them a perfect fit for Mimir. This guide wires up an existing vault and keeps it indexed.

docs.title: 1. Link the vault into notes/
docs.text:
Mimir ingests MIMIR_NOTES_DIR (default ./notes). Rather than copy your vault, symlink it so edits in Obsidian are picked up directly:

ln -s ~/Documents/Obsidian/MyVault notes/vault

You can link multiple vaults or subfolders side by side:

ln -s ~/Documents/Obsidian/Work notes/work
ln -s ~/Documents/Obsidian/Personal notes/personal

Or point Mimir straight at the vault

Skip the symlink and ingest the vault in place:

uv run mimir ingest ~/Documents/Obsidian/MyVault

docs.title: 2. Ingest
docs.text:
uv run mimir ingest

Mimir walks the tree, loads every supported file, chunks it, embeds the chunks, and upserts them into Qdrant. Supported extensions:

.md · .markdown · .txt · .pdf · .rst · .org

docs.title: 3. Query within a folder
docs.text:
Because the relative folder is stored on every chunk, you can scope searches to a single vault or subfolder:

uv run mimir query "weekly review takeaways" --folder work

Other useful filters:

uv run mimir query "fabric sourcing" --ext .md --k 10
uv run mimir query "Avellaneda-Stoikov" --title "market-making.md" --full

docs.title: How incremental ingest works
docs.text:
Each file's content is hashed (SHA-256) and stored alongside its chunks. On the next ingest:

flowchart TD
    file["File on disk"] --> hash["Compute SHA-256"]
    hash --> check{"Hash matches<br/>stored value?"}
    check -->|"yes"| skip["Skip — already indexed"]
    check -->|"no"| reindex["Delete old chunks,<br/>re-embed, upsert"]

So re-running ingest after editing a handful of notes only touches those notes. Use --force to bypass the check and re-embed everything (e.g. after changing the embedding model).

docs.title: Keeping it in sync
docs.text:
For a vault you edit constantly, run the watcher instead of re-ingesting by hand — see Live watch & auto-ingest.

Attachments and non-text files

Images, audio, and other binaries in your vault are ignored. PDFs are parsed page by page; a corrupt page is skipped rather than failing the whole file.

docs.title: Local RAG chat
docs.text: mimir query returns raw matching chunks. mimir chat goes a step further: it retrieves the relevant chunks, feeds them to a local LLM as context, and streams back a written answer with citations. All on your machine.

docs.title: 1. Install and start Ollama
docs.text:
brew install ollama   # macOS; see ollama.com for Linux
ollama pull qwen2.5:7b-instruct

Ollama runs as a background service on http://localhost:11434. If it isn't running, start it with:

ollama serve

docs.title: 2. Ask a question
docs.text:
uv run mimir chat "summarise my notes on tailoring fabric sourcing"

The answer streams token by token, followed by the sources it drew from:

Example output
Asking qwen2.5:7b-instruct via http://localhost:11434...

Your notes identify three preferred mills for suiting cloth, with a
strong preference for high-twist worsteds for travel...

Sources:
  [1] fabric-sourcing.md   notes/work/tailoring/fabric-sourcing.md
  [2] mills-shortlist.md    notes/work/tailoring/mills-shortlist.md

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    q["Your question"] --> embed["Embed query"]
    embed --> retrieve["Retrieve top-k chunks<br/>from Qdrant"]
    retrieve --> prompt["Build grounded prompt"]
    prompt --> llm["Ollama (local LLM)"]
    llm --> answer["Streamed answer<br/>+ citations"]

The system prompt instructs the model to answer using only the provided context and not to invent facts — so answers stay grounded in your notes.

docs.title: Useful flags
docs.text:
# Retrieve more context chunks (default 6)
uv run mimir chat "..." --k 10

# Hide the sources list
uv run mimir chat "..." --no-sources

See the CLI reference for the full list.

docs.title: Choosing a model
docs.text:
Any chat model available to Ollama works. Set it with MIMIR_OLLAMA_MODEL:

MIMIR_OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.1:8b-instruct-q8_0

Larger models give better synthesis at the cost of speed and memory. The default qwen2.5:7b-instruct is a good balance on a 16 GB+ Mac.

Ollama not running?

If chat errors out, make sure the daemon is up (ollama serve) and the model is pulled (ollama pull <model>). query works without Ollama — only chat needs it.

docs.title: Live watch & auto-ingest
docs.text: mimir watch keeps your collection in sync automatically: it runs an initial ingest, then re-ingests whenever a file under the watched path changes.

docs.title: Prerequisite: fswatch
docs.text:
The watcher shells out to fswatch:

brew install fswatch

If fswatch isn't on your PATH, mimir watch exits with an install hint.

docs.title: Run the watcher
docs.text:
uv run mimir watch
Example output
Watching /Users/you/mimir/notes  (Ctrl-C to stop)
change detected — reingesting
change detected — reingesting

Point it at a specific path if you don't want the whole notes directory:

uv run mimir watch notes/work

Stop it with Ctrl+C.

docs.title: How it behaves
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    start["mimir watch"] --> initial["Initial ingest"]
    initial --> listen["fswatch listens<br/>for changes"]
    listen -->|"file saved"| reingest["Incremental re-ingest"]
    reingest --> listen

Each re-ingest is incremental, so only changed files are re-embedded — the same SHA-256 skip logic as a manual ingest. The --latency=2 setting batches rapid saves so a flurry of edits triggers one re-ingest rather than many.

Leave it running in a split pane

Keep mimir watch in one terminal while you write in Obsidian. By the time you switch over to query or chat, your edits are already indexed.

Watch vs. cron

watch is ideal for an active editing session. For a vault you only touch occasionally, a plain mimir ingest on demand is simpler.

docs.title: Reference
docs.text:
The precise details, for when you need them.

  • CLI reference

    Every command, argument, and option — generated directly from the Typer application source.

  • Architecture

    The components and data flow behind ingest, query, and chat.

See also the configuration reference for every MIMIR_* environment variable.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Mimir is a small, local-first pipeline. Notes go in, embeddings get stored in a local vector database, and you query them either as semantic search or as full RAG with a local LLM. Nothing leaves your machine.

flowchart TD
    notes["notes/<br/>(.md .pdf ...)"] --> ingest["ingest<br/>chunk + embed"]
    ingest --> qdrant[("Qdrant<br/>(Docker)")]
    qdrant --> query["query<br/>(semantic)"]
    qdrant --> chat["chat<br/>(RAG)"]
    ollama["Ollama<br/>(local LLM)"] --> chat

docs.title: Components
docs.text: Component Role Qdrant Vector store, a single Docker container. fastembed Embeddings via BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 (384d, ONNX, native on Apple Silicon). Ollama Local LLM for the chat command (optional). Typer The single mimir CLI for everything.

docs.title: Module map
docs.text: Module Responsibility mimir.cli Typer app and all subcommands. mimir.config pydantic-settings configuration from MIMIR_* / .env. mimir.db Qdrant client and collection lifecycle. mimir.embeddings Cached fastembed wrapper (model loaded once per process). mimir.loaders File loaders for .md, .txt, .pdf, .rst, .org. mimir.chunking Paragraph- and sentence-aware sliding-window chunker. mimir.ingest The walk → chunk → embed → upsert pipeline. mimir.query Semantic search and the Hit dataclass. mimir.chat RAG: retrieve → build prompt → stream from Ollama.

docs.title: Data flow
docs.text:
1. Ingest walks the notes directory, chunks each document, embeds the chunks with fastembed, and upserts the vectors into Qdrant. Unchanged files are skipped on re-ingest via a per-file SHA-256 hash.
  2. Query embeds your search text and returns the top-k most similar chunks straight from Qdrant — no LLM involved.
  3. Chat retrieves the relevant chunks, then passes them to a local Ollama model as context, returning an answer with citations back to your notes.

docs.title: Design choices worth knowing
docs.text:
• Deterministic point IDs — each chunk's ID is derived from sha256(path:chunk_idx), so re-upserting the same chunk is idempotent.
  • Pre-built payload indexes on path, ext, folder, title, and file_hash keep filtered queries fast.
  • Cached embedder — the fastembed model is an lru_cache'd singleton, loaded once per process, with a separate query-prefixed path for searches.
  • Strictly grounded RAG — the chat system prompt instructs the model to answer using only the retrieved context, reducing hallucination.

docs.title: CLI reference
docs.text:
Every Mimir command, argument, and option below is generated directly from the Typer application source, so it never drifts out of date.

Help on demand

Every command also accepts --help:

uv run mimir --help
uv run mimir query --help

docs.title: mimir
docs.text:
Local second brain — Qdrant + fastembed + Ollama.

Usage:

mimir [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

docs.title: chat
docs.text:
Ask Mimir using retrieval-augmented generation (requires Ollama running).

Usage:

mimir chat [OPTIONS] TEXT

Options:

  TEXT                      Your question.  [required]
  -k, --k INTEGER           Number of context chunks to retrieve.  [default:
                            6]
  --sources / --no-sources  [default: sources]

docs.title: ingest
docs.text:
Walk PATH, chunk, embed, and upsert into Qdrant.

Usage:

mimir ingest [OPTIONS] [PATH]

Options:

  [PATH]       Path to ingest. Defaults to MIMIR_NOTES_DIR.
  -f, --force  Re-embed even unchanged files.

docs.title: query
docs.text:
Semantic search across your notes.

Usage:

mimir query [OPTIONS] TEXT

Options:

  TEXT             Search query.  [required]
  -k, --k INTEGER  Number of results.  [default: 5]
  --folder TEXT    Filter by folder.
  --ext TEXT       Filter by extension, e.g. .md
  --title TEXT     Filter by exact file title.
  --full           Show full chunk text (no truncation).

docs.title: reset
docs.text:
Drop and recreate the collection. All embeddings are deleted.

Usage:

mimir reset [OPTIONS]

Options:

  -y, --yes  Skip confirmation.

docs.title: status
docs.text:
Show collection stats and current configuration.

Usage:

mimir status [OPTIONS]

docs.title: version
docs.text:
Show Mimir version.

Usage:

mimir version [OPTIONS]

docs.title: watch
docs.text:
Re-ingest whenever files change (requires fswatch).

Usage:

mimir watch [OPTIONS] [PATH]

Options:

  [PATH]  Path to watch. Defaults to MIMIR_NOTES_DIR.
